Three cushion research

Mathavan
Mathavan
Cushion restitution
Han rolling friction
Han sliding friction
Stronge ω ratio
Stronge eₙ
Stronge μ

Mathavan

  • μs (ball-table sliding friction)
    Lower → topspin/backspin/corkscrew take longer to wear off and transition to natural roll.
  • μw (ball-cushion friction)
    Lower → sidespin has less effect changing the rebound angle off the rail, and more sidespin remains on the cueball.

Stronge

  • stronge_e_n (Normal restitution) — higher = springier cushion, less energy lost on direct impacts.
  • stronge_μ (Cushion friction) — lower = slippery cushion, sidespin alters rebound angle less.
  • stronge_omega_ratio (Stiffness ratio) — controls normal/tangential frequency ratio; higher = smoother, more delayed spin transfer.

Han

  • muS (sliding friction) — controls how fast the cue ball "grabs" the cloth with spin.
  • mu (rolling friction) — controls how quickly a rolling ball stops.

Adjust the parameters and share results to discuss. Presets are saved to your browser's local storage. Suggestions? Please open an issue on my GitHub repo.

Play Practice Lobby Leaderboard GitHub Blog: Cushion Physics Blog: Line Up Blog: Mathavan
